steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to explain what chapter 1 specifically covers. The notes state that chapter 1 outlines the origins and use of the term nostalgia — so the correct choice must reference that content and only that chapter.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — origins and use of nostalgia, citing chapter 1: directly matches what the notes say chapter 1 covers. ✓
  • Choice B — cites the wrong chapter: draws from a different chapter's content, not chapter 1. ✗
  • Choice C — mixes multiple chapters: combines content from several chapters rather than focusing on chapter 1 alone. ✗
  • Choice D — generalizes to the whole book: describes the book's overall argument instead of what chapter 1 specifically covers. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
